summary: Tested Privacy Shield, Drawer Upgrade CTA, Stripe Identity, Spot Import (195 spots), and Cache Purge features. All backend tests pass (13/13). Frontend map shows correct clustering (4, 9, 7, 4, 13 for Florida).

minor.issue: Returns 404 - endpoint may not exist or route not registered

critical_code_review_comments: Privacy Shield correctly implemented: active_photographers_count ALWAYS returned (lines 392-399 in surf_spots.py), but active_photographers=[] and breathing_status=false when out of geofence (lines 401-425)
GeofenceUpgradeCTA component shows 'X Pros Shooting Now. Upgrade to Premium to Book' when activePhotographersCount > 0 (lines 420-431 in UnifiedSpotDrawer.js)
Stripe Identity uses STRIPE_API_KEY correctly (line 507 in payments.py)
purgeLocationCache function exists in useIPGeolocation.js (lines 138-163) - clears spot_, nearby_, activity_, photographer_, geofence_ caches on city migration
Map clustering shows correct Florida spot distribution: 4 (Jacksonville), 9 (Daytona), 7 (Space Coast), 4 (Treasure Coast), 13 (Miami/SE Florida)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Privacy Shield geofencing verified - active_photographers_count always returned for upsell, but list/breathing_status nullified when out of range. 195 spots imported. Stripe Identity working with STRIPE_API_KEY. Map clustering shows correct Florida distribution.

privacy_shield_count_always_returned.details: GET /api/surf-spots/{id} always returns active_photographers_count regardless of geofence status

privacy_shield_nullification.details: When out of geofence: active_photographers=[], breathing_status=false, live_conditions_report=null

drawer_upgrade_cta.details: GeofenceUpgradeCTA shows 'X Pros Shooting Now. Upgrade to Premium to Book' when activePhotographersCount > 0

stripe_identity.details: POST /api/payments/identity/create-session uses STRIPE_API_KEY, returns session_id starting with 'vs_'

spot_import_195.details: Total spots count is 195 (verified via API and CURATED_SPOTS count)

cache_purge.details: purgeLocationCache function exists in useIPGeolocation.js, clears location-based caches on city migration

map_clustering.details: Florida spots clustered correctly: 4, 9, 7, 4, 13 visible on map
